Article Title: Brazil vs Scotland could face major World Cup disruption as FIFA prepares to enforce strict protocol
Article Text:
FILE - Brazil players pose for a team photo before an international friendly soccer game against Croatia, Tuesday, March 31, 2026, in Orlando, Fla. (AP Photo/Kevin Kolczynski, File)

Qualification stakes make the timing particularly significant

Thunderstorm forecast raises concerns in Miami

A sign advising fans of a weather delay is displayed during the World Cup Group I soccer match between France and Iraq in Philadelphia, Monday, June 22, 2026. (AP Photo/Matt Rourke)

What FIFA's weather protocol says

Clarke says Scotland are already prepared

Scotland's decisive Group C clash against Brazil at the 2026 FIFA World Cup could become the second match of the tournament to face major weather disruption, with forecasts warning of thunderstorms in Miami just hours before kick-off and FIFA's strict lightning protocols potentially coming into effect.The final round of Group C fixtures carries significant qualification implications for both nations. Brazil arrive in a strong position to reach the Round of 32, while Scotland are chasing a place in the knockout stages of a major tournament for the first time in their history.With severe weather expected across parts of Florida on Wednesday evening, attention has shifted away from the teams and towards the possibility of another lengthy interruption similar to the one that affected France's victory over Iraq earlier this week.Scotland opened their World Cup campaign with a 1-0 victory over Haiti, securing the nation's first World Cup win since 1990. However, Steve Clarke's side followed that result with a narrow 1-0 defeat against Morocco, leaving their qualification hopes dependent on the outcome against Brazil.A victory would guarantee Scotland a place in the Round of 32 and could even see them finish top of the group if Morocco fail to beat Haiti.A draw would move Scotland onto four points and is widely expected to be enough to secure progression as one of the tournament's best third-placed teams.Even defeat would not automatically eliminate Scotland, although their fate would then depend on results elsewhere and comparisons with third-placed teams from the other groups.Brazil's position is considerably stronger. The five-time world champions opened with a 1-1 draw against Morocco before defeating Haiti 3-0 in their second match.A win or draw against Scotland would secure automatic qualification, while even defeat would be unlikely to prevent progression given Brazil's superior goal difference and strong position among the potential third-placed qualifiers.According to forecasts, Miami could experience thunderstorms close to kick-off time.Scotland's match against Brazil is scheduled to begin at 6pm local time, while weather projections indicate a potential thunderstorm could hit the region at approximately 5pm. Accuweather has placed the probability of precipitation at 54 per cent, while a moderate heat warning also remains in effect across Florida.The concern comes only days after the first weather-related disruption of the tournament occurred during France's Group I match against Iraq in Philadelphia.That match was suspended at half-time after lightning strikes were detected near the stadium. Players and supporters were forced to seek shelter before play eventually resumed more than two hours later. France ultimately completed a 3-0 victory after a delay that stretched well beyond the minimum waiting period.Heavy rainfall also affected Norway's 3-2 victory over Senegal in New Jersey, although that match was completed without interruption.The procedures being used throughout the tournament are based on United States government weather regulations and are mandatory for FIFA.If lightning or electrical discharge is detected within an eight-mile radius of a stadium, play must be suspended immediately.Players are taken back to the dressing rooms, while supporters are directed to designated shelter areas inside the venue.Once a lightning strike is recorded, a 30-minute countdown begins. If no further strikes are detected during that period, the match can resume.However, every additional lightning strike resets the clock back to zero, meaning delays can quickly extend for hours if storm activity continues around the stadium.FIFA has also identified three warning indicators when assessing severe weather conditions: visible lightning, audible thunder, and skies considered threatening enough to create a safety risk.There is currently no specific FIFA regulation that determines when a match must be abandoned due to weather, with each situation assessed on a case-by-case basis.Scotland manager Steve Clarke revealed that his squad has already discussed the possibility of disruption and has contingency plans in place should the match be delayed."Looking at the forecast tomorrow, it's possible that there will be some rain disruptions," Clarke said during his pre-match press conference."We have a strategy in mind on how to deal with it. Obviously, you don't know how long the delay is going to be. If there is to be a delay then hopefully it can be a short one."We have got a coping strategy and an idea what we would do if that happened to us."For now, organisers will hope the weather clears before kick-off. But after France versus Iraq became the first World Cup match affected by lightning, Brazil versus Scotland has emerged as the next fixture that could test FIFA's severe weather protocols at a tournament being played during North America's peak storm season.
Article Summary: Scotland's decisive Group C clash against Brazil could be affected by severe weather. Forecasts warn of thunderstorms in Miami just hours before kick-off. FIFA's strict lightning protocols potentially come into effect. Scotland manager Steve Clarke says his side have already discussed the possibility of disruption.

Article Title: Why Jude Bellingham was not sent off despite breaking World Cup's most debated new rule
Article Text:
Jude Bellingham triggers confusion over FIFA's new rule during England's World Cup draw/ Image: X

What does FIFA's new mouth-covering rule actually say?

Why Bellingham was not punished

How Miguel Almirón's red card was different

Bellingham also addresses clash with Ghana bench

England midfielder Jude Bellingham became the centre of a fresh World Cup controversy during Tuesday's goalless draw with Ghana after television cameras captured him covering his mouth while speaking to Ghana captain Jordan Ayew. With FIFA's new mouth-covering rule already producing the tournament's first red card, many viewers questioned why the England star escaped punishment.The incident occurred during England's frustrating 0-0 draw against Ghana in Boston, a result that left Group L finely balanced heading into the final round of fixtures.Bellingham's interaction with Ayew quickly attracted attention because FIFA introduced a new regulation ahead of the 2026 World Cup allowing players to be sent off if they cover their mouths while confronting opponents.However, the key word in the law is confrontation.The rule was introduced after FIFA president Gianni Infantino pushed for stricter measures following an incident involving Benfica winger Gianluca Prestianni, who received a six-match UEFA ban for homophobic conduct towards Real Madrid forward Vinícius Júnior during a Champions League match earlier this year.Ahead of the World Cup, FIFA's head of referees, Pierluigi Collina, made it clear that covering the mouth itself was not prohibited."Players can continue to cover their mouth with an arm and the shirt because they may chat with friends," Collina explained before the tournament."It's normal to chat before, during or after the match."So if the conversation is a friendly conversation, they can continue to do it without any problem."When the conversation is confrontational, covering the mouth means that you are doing something very wrong, potentially, and the sanction is the red card."That distinction ultimately proved crucial in Bellingham's case.Although images of the England midfielder speaking to Ayew circulated widely, there was no indication that the exchange was hostile or confrontational.Sources told ESPN that Bellingham's discussion with the Ghana captain was not deemed aggressive, meaning the incident did not meet the threshold required for disciplinary action under FIFA's new regulation.Players, coaches and even referees have regularly been seen covering their mouths during conversations throughout the tournament. FIFA's concern is specifically focused on situations involving arguments, confrontations or potentially abusive language.As a result, neither referee Hector Said Martinez Sorto nor the video assistant referee considered the incident worthy of review.The comparison most frequently made involved Paraguay forward Miguel Almirón, who became the first player in World Cup history to be sent off under the new rule.Almirón was dismissed during Paraguay's Group D clash against Türkiye following a heated confrontation involving Turkish defender Mert Müldür.The incident occurred after Paraguay's Isidro Pitta went down following a challenge from Ismail Yüksek, triggering a melee between both sets of players. During the confrontation, Almirón covered his mouth while speaking to Müldür.Following a VAR review, officials deemed the situation confrontational and issued a red card.Speaking after that incident, Infantino reinforced FIFA's position."This thing about covering the mouth is for us a very, very important rule," he said."It's about respect. It's about the example that we should give."If you have nothing to hide, you don't cover your mouth when you speak to somebody."The rules have been made very clear to everyone."While the conversation with Ayew was deemed harmless, Bellingham was involved in a separate flashpoint during the match that began at half-time and carried into the second half.The Real Madrid midfielder became embroiled in a heated, foul-mouthed exchange with members of Ghana's coaching staff, including manager Carlos Queiroz and assistant John Paintsil, after a strong challenge on Ghana defender Jerome Opoku. The confrontation initially erupted at half-time following the reckless tackle, with tensions spilling over as both benches reacted angrily before continuing during the match.Speaking afterwards, Bellingham admitted he had mistimed the tackle but insisted the disagreement stemmed from the competitive nature of the match."I made a silly tackle to be honest. I was trying to win the ball and I followed through a little bit and caught the lad, I spoke to him after and then their bench jumped up trying to get me a yellow card," Bellingham said."So yeah I think their manager, I just recognized him, was obviously the one who used to be at Manchester United [Carlos Queiroz], so great respect and nothing but a competitive edge for both of us."
Article Summary: Jude Bellingham was caught on camera covering his mouth while speaking to Ghana captain Jordan Ayew during England's 0-0 draw with Ghana. FIFA introduced a new rule allowing players to be sent off if they cover their mouths while confronting opponents. The rule was introduced after FIFA president Gianni Infantino pushed for stricter measures following an incident involving Gianluca Prestianni.

Article Title: FIFA president reveals Donald Trump's major role at 2026 World Cup final in surprise announcement
Article Text:
FILE - FIFA President Gianni Infantino presents President Donald Trump with the FIFA Peace Prize during the draw for the 2026 soccer World Cup at the Kennedy Center in Washington, Friday, Dec. 5, 2025. (AP Photo/Stephanie Scarbrough, Pool, File_

Break from recent World Cup protocol

Relationship between Trump and Infantino

FILE - President Donald Trump speaks alongside the FIFA World Cup Winners Trophy as FIFA President Gianni Infantino, right, and Vice President JD Vance, left, watch in the Oval Office of the White House, Aug. 22, 2025, in Washington. (AP Photo/Jacquelyn Martin, File)

Club World Cup presentation sparked controversy

FIFA president Gianni Infantino has confirmed that United States President Donald Trump will join him in presenting the FIFA World Cup trophy to the winners of the 2026 final, marking a departure from recent tournament tradition and placing the US president at the centre of the competition's showpiece event.With the World Cup entering its decisive stages, attention is already beginning to shift towards the final on July 19 at New York New Jersey Stadium. While the identity of the finalists remains unknown, FIFA has now revealed who will be handing over the trophy.Speaking during an interview on Fox & Friends, Infantino confirmed that Trump will share the presentation duties when the tournament concludes."We will be together with the president enjoying the final and handing the trophy to the winner, of course," Infantino said.When asked whether the pair would jointly present the trophy, the FIFA president responded: "Of course, we are together all the time."The decision represents a notable shift from recent World Cup finals.During the 2018 World Cup in Russia and the 2022 tournament in Qatar, the trophy was presented by Infantino himself. His predecessor, Sepp Blatter, also oversaw trophy presentations during his tenure as FIFA president.However, football's history includes several examples of heads of state and monarchs taking part in the ceremony.Following Italy's World Cup triumph in 1982, Spain's King Juan Carlos handed the trophy to captain Dino Zoff. England captain Bobby Moore famously received the Jules Rimet Trophy from Queen Elizabeth II after England's victory at the 1966 World Cup.The 2026 final will therefore revive a tradition that has occasionally seen political and royal figures play a prominent role in football's biggest moment.Infantino and Trump have maintained a close working relationship throughout the build-up to the tournament.While the FIFA president has been a constant presence at matches across the United States, Canada and Mexico, Trump has kept a relatively low profile during the competition itself and has not yet attended a match.According to reports, Infantino has attempted to attend as many fixtures as possible during the tournament, often appearing at multiple matches on the same day.The World Cup final, however, will see both men together on football's biggest stage.The announcement also comes less than a year after Trump's involvement in the FIFA Club World Cup final created significant discussion.Following Chelsea's victory over Paris Saint-Germain, Trump presented the trophy to Chelsea captain Reece James but then remained on the winners' podium during the team's celebrations.The moment appeared to catch several Chelsea players by surprise."They told me that he was going to present the trophy and then exit the stage and I thought that he was going to exit the stage, but he wanted to stay," James said afterwards.Asked what Trump had said to him, James replied: "To be honest it was quite loud. I couldn't hear too much. He just congratulated me and the team for lifting the trophy and told us to enjoy the moment."Chelsea star Cole Palmer also admitted he had not expected Trump to remain on stage during the celebrations."I knew he was going to be here, but I didn't know he was going to be on the stand when we lifted the trophy. I was a bit confused, yes," Palmer said.The 2026 FIFA World Cup final will take place on July 19 at New York New Jersey Stadium, bringing the expanded 48-team tournament to a close.Whoever emerges as world champions will now receive the trophy from both FIFA president Gianni Infantino and United States President Donald Trump, ensuring that one of football's most iconic moments will also carry significant political and historical attention.
Article Summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino has confirmed that United States President Donald Trump will join him in presenting the FIFA World Cup trophy to the winners of the 2026 final. The decision marks a departure from recent tournament tradition and puts the US president at the centre of the competition's showpiece event. Trump's involvement in the FIFA Club World Cup final created significant discussion.

Article Title: Quarterback Season 3 cast locked in: Netflix finally reveals which NFL QBs made the final cut
Article Text:
Quarterback Season 3 cast locked in: Netflix reveals which NFL QBs made the final cut(Getty)

Netflix Quarterback Season 3 puts four very different NFL journeys under the spotlight

Netflix has officially made its call. Recently, the streaming giant dropped the first trailer for Season 3 of Quarterback and revealed the four NFL quarterbacks who made the final lineup. Like the last two seasons, the series will track the highs, lows, and pressure points of life under center.This year’s cast brings a different kind of energy. Baker Mayfield, Jayden Daniels , Joe Flacco, and Cam Ward all enter with very different stakes. Some are fighting to stay on top. Others are trying to prove they belong.That is what makes this season stand out right away. The first two seasons leaned heavily on established stars. This one feels more unpredictable. There is no Patrick Mahomes-level lock here. Instead, Netflix went with four quarterbacks, each carrying a different kind of pressure, which could make the storytelling stronger.Baker Mayfield enters as the group's proven fighter. The Tampa Bay Buccaneers quarterback has spent years bouncing between doubt and redemption. In 2025, he kept Tampa in the playoff race and once again showed why teams keep betting on him.He plays with emotion, and that always spills into moments cameras love. When things get heated on the sideline or inside the locker room, Mayfield rarely hides it.Then comes Jayden Daniels, and his story may be the most important one. After his breakout rookie season with the Washington Commanders, the expectations got much louder in 2025. Defenses adjusted. The spotlight got bigger. Then injuries slowed him down. Netflix had cameras around during that stretch, which means fans will likely see the pressure that comes with trying to live up to early hype.Joe Flacco brings the veteran angle. At 41, his NFL story looked close to over. Then came another shot. That is why his inclusion feels different. In the official trailer released by Netflix, Joe Flacco says, “You’re not always in charge of how things go. You just have to be willing to accept challenges and see where they take you.” He says it while reflecting on the twists of his career, and it fits his story perfectly.And then there is Cam Ward, the rookie wildcard. As the No. 1 overall pick, he entered the Tennessee Titans with massive pressure. Rookie quarterbacks often struggle, but Ward’s season came with coaching noise, losses, and heavy expectations from day one. That kind of chaos can shape a career fast.Quarterback Season 3 drops on July 14. This time, Netflix is not selling perfection. It is selling pressure, setbacks, and survival. That is exactly why these four made the cut.
Article Summary: Quarterback Season 3 drops on July 14. Baker Mayfield, Jayden Daniels, Joe Flacco, and Cam Ward all enter with very different stakes. This time, Netflix is not selling perfection. It is selling pressure, setbacks, and survival.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles linked to $36M Cleveland Browns defensive star amid rebuild buzz
Article Text:
NFL Trade Rumors: Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles linked to $36M Browns defensive star amid Cleveland rebuild buzz(Getty)

Category



Details



Player Name



Grant Delpit



Current Team



Cleveland Browns



Rumored Teams



Kansas City Chiefs, Philadelphia Eagles



Contract Status



Under contract (final active year)



Current Deal



3 years, $36 million



Trade Likelihood



Moderate

$8.03 million



Latest Insider Update



Anthony Palacios suggests Cleveland could move him before needing a new extension



Potential Return



Third-round pick





Which teams are interested in Grant Delpit?

What insiders are saying about the trade rumors?

Contract details and salary cap implications

How could the trade impact both teams?

The Cleveland Browns may not be finished reshaping their roster. After already moving on from key veterans this offseason and entering what looks like a soft reset, trade chatter is now starting to build around veteran safety Grant Delpit. The 28-year-old is entering a crucial contract year, and with no extension in place, league insiders believe Cleveland could listen if the right offer comes.Delpit signed a three-year, $36 million extension back in 2023, but his future beyond this season remains unclear. While the Browns still value him, the front office has younger options developing and may not be eager to hand out another expensive deal during a transitional phase.The Kansas City Chiefs have emerged as one of the cleaner fits. The Chiefs have spent much of the offseason rebuilding pieces of their secondary, and while they brought back familiar talent, there are still questions at safety. Delpit would bring versatility, physicality, and playoff-ready experience to a defense that is still built to compete for championships.The Philadelphia Eagles are another team quietly tied to the rumors.Philadelphia lost important defensive depth this offseason and has been aggressive in plugging holes before. Delpit’s ability to play deep, rotate into the box, and blitz would fit naturally in Vic Fangio’s structure. With their championship window still wide open, adding another experienced defensive back would make sense.Anthony Palacios of Last Word on Sports believes Delpit is one of the more realistic trade candidates on the Cleveland Browns roster right now. Palacios pointed to two major factors: Delpit’s contract situation and Cleveland’s confidence in younger safeties already on the roster. His report suggests the Browns could avoid another expensive extension by moving him now while his value is still strong. That is the key here. This is not just random speculation. It is tied directly to Cleveland’s roster timeline and financial decisions.Grant Delpit’s current three-year, $36 million contract carries an $8.03 million cap hit for 2026, while his cash payout this year rises to $12 million. That is crucial because if the Cleveland Browns want to keep him beyond this season, his next deal will almost certainly cost more. The safety market has climbed steadily, and Delpit’s age and versatility could push his next contract into the $14–16 million per year range.For contenders like the Kansas City Chiefs or the Philadelphia Eagles, that creates an interesting opportunity. They would get a proven starting safety for one manageable season and could decide later whether to extend him.For the Kansas City Chiefs, adding Delpit could strengthen a defense that may need to carry more of the burden if the offense takes time to find rhythm. His range and tackling ability would immediately improve the backend.For the Philadelphia Eagles, it would be another aggressive all-in move. The Eagles have never been shy about adding proven veterans when chasing another Super Bowl, and Delpit would give them flexibility across the secondary.For the Cleveland Browns, trading Delpit would signal a stronger commitment to the future. It would save them from another major contract decision and allow younger talent to take over while adding valuable draft capital. With training camp approaching, this is one rumor worth watching closely.
Article Summary: Cleveland Browns safety Grant Delpit has been linked to the Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles. Delpit is entering a crucial contract year, and with no extension in place, league insiders believe Cleveland could listen if the right offer comes. The 28-year-old signed a three-year, $36 million extension back in 2023.

Article Title: George Kittle exposes Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's surprise wedding rule that even close friends must obey
Article Text:
George Kittle Plans to Break Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's "No Gifts" Wedding Rule (Gregg DeGuire/Variety via Getty; Bruce Glikas/WireImage)

What did George Kittle say about Taylor Swift at Tight End University?

What are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans?

San Francisco 49ers tight end George Kittle is not exactly planning to follow the rules at Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce 's wedding. Speaking to ExtraTV at the Tight Ends and Friends Concert in Nashville on Tuesday, the 32-year-old confirmed his invitation to the upcoming ceremony and casually admitted he is already thinking about bending one particular instruction. Swift and Kelce reportedly told guests no gifts, which Kittle found amusing but not entirely binding. "They said absolutely no gifts. But I was thinking, Travis, for some reason, really likes old coins, so I might get him an old coin," the San Francisco 49ers tight end said, joking, "Sounds expensive, too."George Kittle's appearance at Tight End University in Nashville gave him more than just a chance to mentor the league's best pass-catchers. It gave him a front-row seat to just how much Swift brings to the people around her, even with her wedding days away.Taylor Swift made a surprise appearance at the Pinnacle on Tuesday night and joined Lainey Wilson for a duet that drew the loudest reaction of the evening. Before the duet, Swift joked it was a "special request by a very special tight end" and pointed directly at Kittle. His mother was apparently there, and Swift made time to speak with her too."She's just hanging out, talking to my mom. Shout out to Taylor for talking to my mom again. But she's just so kind, and it's awesome to have her around," Kittle said.Tight End University, co-founded in 2021 by Travis Kelce, Kittle, and Greg Olsen, is a three-day summit held annually in Nashville. It combines film sessions, on-field work, and recovery programming, with the concert serving as its biggest public-facing moment and directing proceeds toward causes championed by all three founders.Kittle made clear that Swift's presence carried extra weight this year. "She's just such an awesome person to give us her time this close to their wedding," he said. "So we're just so thankful that they're here."Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce announced their engagement in August 2025, and the summer wedding speculation has been building steadily since. Reports have linked the couple to a July ceremony at Madison Square Garden, though neither side has officially confirmed a date or venue.In the days leading up to TEU, both spent time with their closest circles. Swift was spotted at her Watch Hill mansion in Rhode Island with longtime friend Abigail Anderson and other close friends for what appeared to be a girls' weekend. Kelce, meanwhile, was seen around California with what looked like a bachelor party group that included his brother Jason, Patrick Mahomes, comedian Druski, and Swift's own brother Austin.As for Kittle, he is also managing his own timeline. He is recovering from an Achilles tear suffered during the Wild Card loss to the Philadelphia Eagles in January 2026 and targeting a Week 1 return against the Los Angeles Rams.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce reportedly told guests no gifts at their wedding. San Francisco 49ers tight end George Kittle plans to break that rule. Kittle was speaking at Tight End University in Nashville on Tuesday. Swift made a surprise appearance at the concert and sang with Lainey Wilson.

Article Title: Dianna Russini finally addresses Mike Vrabel scandal: "Significant impact on my life"
Article Text:
Dianna Russini Says Vrabel Scandal Has Upended Her Life as NYT Investigation Drags On (Getty Images)

How has the Mike Vrabel scandal affected Dianna Russini's career?

What did Dianna Russini tell the New York Times and why does it matter?

Dianna Russini has broken her silence. The former Athletic NFL insider told the New York Times that the fallout from her relationship with New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel has taken a serious toll, both professionally and personally. Russini, who resigned from her $800,000-a-year role earlier this year amid the controversy, briefly spoke to the Times, which is currently conducting its own investigation into the matter.The damage to Russini's career has been considerable. She left The Athletic before her contract expired on June 30, citing what she called an unbearable "media frenzy" that was spiralling beyond her control."This media frenzy is hurtling forward without regard for the review process The Athletic is trying to complete," Russini wrote in her resignation letter. "Rather than allowing this to continue, I have decided to step aside now. I do so not because I accept the narrative that has been constructed around this episode, but because I refuse to lend it further oxygen or to let it define me or my career."She had reportedly been in discussions for a new contract at The Athletic when the first set of Page Six photos dropped in April, showing her and Vrabel at a luxury resort in Arizona.Those images sparked immediate public backlash since both are married to other people. Russini and Vrabel denied any romantic involvement and claimed friends were with them on the trip, but eyewitnesses disputed that account.Things escalated from there. On the first day of the 2026 NFL Draft, Page Six published a second batch of photos, this time showing the pair kissing at a New York City bar in 2020. That was six years before the Arizona photos surfaced, and notably, Russini was just six months away from marrying Shake Shack executive Kevin Goldschmidt at the time. Vrabel was already married to his wife, Jen.Vrabel, 50, announced shortly after that he would skip part of the draft to enter counseling. TMZ later reported a boat outing between the two in June 2021, when Russini was pregnant with her first child.When the Times reached out to Dianna Russini for comment ahead of publishing their investigation, she responded via text message but asked not to be quoted. The reporter informed her that since the exchange was never declared off the record, her comments would be included regardless.Russini then emailed The Athletic's publisher and two senior Times editors in an attempt to block her quotes from being printed. It did not work."This has had a significant impact on my life, both professionally and personally," she told the Times.Notably, she referred to herself as a "former journalist" in her communication, a pointed acknowledgment of how sharply her career has shifted. The investigation is still ongoing, and per Front Office Sports' Michael McCarthy, no conclusion is imminent. Some inside the Times building are reportedly growing impatient with the timeline, though top editors have reiterated it is still weeks away.Whether Russini returns to sports media in any meaningful capacity likely depends on what that report ultimately reveals.
Article Summary: Dianna Russini spoke to the New York Times about the Mike Vrabel scandal. She resigned from her $800,000-a-year role at The Athletic earlier this year. The Times is currently conducting its own investigation into the matter.

Article Title: Taylor Swift's wedding ambitions are off the charts and insiders say she wants every guest completely stunned
Article Text:
Travis Kelce & Taylor Swift’s Wedding (Getty Images)

What will Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding look like?

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are planning a wedding that insiders are calling nothing short of extraordinary. Reports from RadarOnline suggest the couple is eyeing Madison Square Garden as a potential venue, with a guest list that could stretch to 1,200 people. The budget, sources claim, may cross $20 million. And according to those close to the planning, the venue itself is only a fraction of what is being prepared.Putting together a guest list of that size, when you are both a global pop star and an NFL champion, is no small task. One insider told RadarOnline that narrowing down names has been a genuinely "painful decision." "Travis and Taylor are both connected to so many people that they hated the idea of anyone feeling left out," the source said. Yet despite the enormous headcount, the couple reportedly wanted to avoid turning their wedding into what insiders described as a "phony Hollywood circus. "The names already linked to the guest list suggest that tension is hard to balance. Selena Gomez is reportedly set to lead the bridal party, joined by husband Benny Blanco. Gigi Hadid, Suki Waterhouse, Ed Sheeran, the Haim sisters, and Cara Delevingne are also among those reportedly invited.But the real conversation has shifted toward what happens once guests actually walk through the doors. Sources suggest the finest food, drink, and entertainment are all being arranged, and Taylor Swift herself has apparently made clear that expectations should not stop at the venue announcement. "Taylor has said that if people think the choice of venue is over the top, they're really going to be blown away by everything they have planned," one insider alleged.The most intriguing detail being floated is the possibility of a live concert featuring Swift's musician friends. It is the kind of addition that would turn an already extravagant wedding into something closer to a ticketed event. Still, the source was careful to note that Taylor Swift "won't confirm or deny" the speculation, which means the biggest details remain, for now, deliberately unconfirmed.Whether Madison Square Garden ends up as the final venue or not, what is becoming clear is that Swift and Kelce are not approaching this quietly. A 1,200-person guest list alone signals that this wedding was never going to be a low-key affair. The only real question left seems to be just how far beyond expectations the two of them are willing to go.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are reportedly planning a $20 million wedding. The couple is reportedly eyeing Madison Square Garden as a potential venue. The guest list could stretch to 1,200 people. Selena Gomez is reportedly set to lead the bridal party.

Article Title: "Cheated on her husband with the kids karate teacher": Tom Brady’s ex Gisele Bundchen reignites old scandal as Barstool analyst drops explosive cheating claim
Article Text:
Tom Brady and Gisele Bundchen’s past relationship is back in the spotlight after Barstool analyst Dante Deiana criticized the supermodel and revived allegations surrounding their split. The comments came as social media users debated Bundchen’s recent tribute to her husband, Joaquim Valente. While some fans defended Brady and questioned Bundchen’s actions, others argued she had no obligation to acknowledge her ex. Brady has not publicly responded to the controversy.

Tom Brady and Gisele Bundchen controversy resurfaces after Father's Day post

Fans split over Tom Brady and Gisele Bundchen Father's Day debate

Tom Brady and Gisele Bundchen may have split up years ago, but public interest in their relationship is as strong as ever. Old debates are back and reactions from fans, media personalities and long-time followers of the former couple are pouring in after Bundchen’s recent Father’s Day post.What started as a heartfelt tribute quickly turned into a social media flashpoint. The post centered on Bundchen's current husband, Joaquim Valente, but many observers noticed one name missing. That omission was enough to spark criticism and reopen discussions surrounding one of the most talked-about celebrity breakups in sports history.Bundchen celebrated Father's Day by honoring Valente, whom she married after her divorce from Brady. Her message praised his character and the example he sets for their family."Happy Father’s Day (Joaquim Valente)! Thank you for leading by example, and for the values you embody, love, humility, integrity, discipline, kindness and consistency. You are an incredible role model. We are all so grateful for you, and we love you so much. ... Happy Father’s Day to all the dads out there. ❤️,"While the post was intended as a tribute, many social media users questioned why Brady was not mentioned.The seven-time Super Bowl champion shares two children with Bundchen and remains a major part of their lives.The reaction intensified when Barstool analyst Dante Deiana publicly defended Brady while criticizing Bundchen. His comments quickly circulated across social media platforms and became a major talking point among NFL fans."Wait, who posted this? The devoted mother and wife who cheated on her husband with the kids karate teacher? Gotcha,"The accusation references rumors that have followed Bundchen since her split from Brady. However, Bundchen has repeatedly denied allegations that she was unfaithful during her marriage.As the discussion gained momentum, fans found themselves divided into two camps. Some believed Brady deserved acknowledgment on Father's Day because of his role as the father of Bundchen's children.One commenter wrote:"Don't forget the father of your other kids. He helped raise them too,"Another added:"Congrats on downgrading,"A third simply reacted:"Nah this is crazy,"Yet not everyone agreed with the criticism. Many defended Bundchen's decision and argued that Father's Day posts are personal choices rather than public obligations.One fan pointed to Brady's past priorities, writing:"Insane that bro gave up his family for one more year of football,"Another added:"Guys it’s not weird to not wish your ex a happy Father’s Day. I sure as hell don’t,"Others praised Valente's role in the family, with one commenter stating:"The Step Father ❌️ The Father that Stepped up ✅️,"Brady stayed silent throughout the debate. The NFL legend did not share a public Father's Day message on Instagram or X, choosing instead to keep the occasion private.Even years after their divorce, reactions to Brady and Bundchen continue to dominate online conversations. The latest controversy shows that whenever either of them posts about family, fans remain ready to revisit old wounds, take sides, and keep the story alive.
Article Summary: Bundchen celebrated Father's Day by honoring her husband, Joaquim Valente. Many social media users questioned why Brady was not mentioned in the post. Some fans defended Brady and questioned Bundchen's actions. Others argued she had no obligation to acknowledge her ex.

Article Title: NFL trade rumors: Shedeur Sanders' future looks uncertain if Deshaun Watson starts
Article Text:
(Image via Getty: Shedeur Sanders and Deshaun Watson)

Could the Browns really trade Shedeur Sanders?



Why Dillon Gabriel could also be on the move



The Cleveland Browns' quarterback battle between Shedeur Sanders and Deshaun Watson has been one of the NFL's most-watched offseason storylines, and now trade rumours are entering the mix. According to Cleveland Plain Dealer reporter Mary Kay Cabot, the Browns have not ruled out trading Sanders if Watson ultimately wins the starting job. Cleveland could also explore moving on from backup Dillon Gabriel, with the team unlikely to keep four quarterbacks on the roster heading into the 2026 season. Nothing is close to a decision yet, but the chatter shows just how unresolved this competition still is.The trade buzz picked up after talks and calls reportedly came in about Sanders' availability, prompting NFL Network's Cameron Wolfe to suggest Cleveland could consider moving him if Watson wins the competition outright.But Cabot has been quick to temper expectations, stating plainly that nothing is happening with it right now and that there's nothing new to report during the league's summer break. Her message has been consistent: Sanders and Watson will battle it out properly once training camp begins, and no decision will be rushed before then.Still, the fact that rival teams are even making calls says plenty about how the league viewsSanders isn't the only quarterback whose future in Cleveland is uncertain.Cabot has suggested the Browns could look to trade Gabriel around the roster cutdown date, particularly if backup Taylen Green proves capable of handling situational snaps like short-yardage and four-minute offense. The logic is simple: Cleveland is extremely unlikely to carry four quarterbacks into the regular season, so whoever doesn't earn a clear role becomes expendable.This is also where things get more complicated for the Browns. If Cleveland adds another arm through the supplemental draft, the team would almost certainly need to move either Sanders or Gabriel to make room.With Watson holding a $230 million investment from the franchise and Sanders showing real flashes late last season, including a 3-4 record as a starter while the team went 2-8 in games he didn't start, there's no easy answer here, just a quarterback room that's about to get a lot more crowded before camp settles it.
Article Summary: Cleveland Browns have not ruled out trading Shedeur Sanders if Deshaun Watson wins the starting job. The team could also explore moving on from backup Dillon Gabriel, with the team unlikely to keep four quarterbacks on the roster heading into the 2026 season.

Article Title: Austin Reaves contract update: Lakers star ends free agency with massive deal to reunite with Luka Doncic
Article Text:
Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves. Image via: AP

Austin Reaves rejoins the Lakers with a whopping deal

Contract Detail



Value



Team



Los Angeles Lakers



Contract Length



4 years



Total Value



$185 million



Average Annual Value



$46.25 million



Previous Option Declined



$14.9 million player option (2026-27)



Notable Achievement



Largest contract ever for a former undrafted player





What Austin Reaves said about rejoining Luka Doncic



Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves. Image via: Imagn Images

Austin Reaves contract update arrived on Wednesday when the Los Angeles Lakers secured one of the biggest priorities of their offseason, agreeing to a four-year, $185 million contract with the breakout guard. The deal ends Reaves' first experience with NBA free agency and keeps one of the franchise's cornerstone players in Los Angeles alongside Luka Doncic.The agreement carries immediate significance for a Lakers team looking to remain in the championship picture. After career-best production and reported interest from rival teams, including the Detroit Pistons and Brooklyn Nets, Reaves chose to remain with the organization where he developed from an undrafted prospect into an All-Star caliber player.For Austin Reaves, the resolution brought relief after months of uncertainty. Speaking to The Athletic's Dan Woike, the Lakers guard admitted the free-agent process was far from enjoyable."I didn't like it," Reaves told The Athletic's Dan Woike. "It was weird. It just was, I mean, it's just a lot of 'What ifs.' I just like to know where I'm going to be."The uncertainty lingered throughout negotiations, even as interest around the league continued to grow.Reaves entered the market after a season in which he averaged 23.3 points, 5.5 assists and 4.7 rebounds despite battling injuries. His production transformed him from a valuable complementary piece into one of the most sought-after free agents available."I mean, it was a breath of fresh air because I hadn't stopped thinking about where I was going to be. And it's just like a sigh of relief," he said in a phone interview on Wednesday. "I mean, regardless of whatever played out, I'm obviously grateful and in a good position to take care of the people I love. My heart's been in L.A. Every morning I wake up, I'm just like, 'I hope we can get this done today.'"And even if I was frustrated through the process when we (weren't) getting the numbers we wanted, the next morning I woke up, it was still the same thing: 'Hopefully we can get this done today.'"The Lakers' decision to retain Reaves was about more than continuity. It was about preserving a partnership they believe can anchor the franchise's next era.With LeBron James' future still drawing attention, Los Angeles has already secured two foundational pieces in Reaves and Doncic. Reaves made it clear that the long-term objective extends beyond contracts and accolades.“It’s going to be fun. And like I said, winning a championship is the main goal,” Reaves said. “And I feel like, you know, with me and Luka, that’s a great starting piece to compete. And when we put a roster together that can go compete, I feel like winning a championship will basically solidify everything that I want and everything that the Lakers want.“And it kind of meets the common goal of why they gave me the money they did.”That statement offers perhaps the clearest indication of the Lakers' next challenge. The franchise has secured one of the league's premier guards for the foreseeable future. The focus now shifts to building the roster around Reaves and Doncic to determine whether this new partnership can deliver the championship both sides now openly expect.
Article Summary: The Los Angeles Lakers signed guard Austin Reaves to a four-year, $185 million contract on Wednesday. The deal ends Reaves' first experience with NBA free agency and keeps one of the franchise's cornerstone players in Los Angeles. Reaves chose to remain with the organization where he developed from an undrafted prospect into an All-Star caliber player.

Article Title: NBA Draft 2026: Knicks pull off savvy draft-day move and land extra future pick
Article Text:
New York Knicks. Image via: Dustin Safranek-Imagn Images

Knicks turn two picks into a bigger draft haul in Rockets deal



Rockets receive



Knicks receive



No. 31 overall (Bruce Thornton)



No. 39 overall (TBD)



No. 55 overall (TBD)



No. 53 overall (TBD)





2029 second-round pick (via Sacramento)





Why the Knicks' draft strategy could impact their offseason plans



The New York Knicks continued their aggressive reshaping of draft assets Thursday night, striking another deal during the second round of the 2026 NBA Draft. According to ESPN's Shams Charania, New York traded the No. 31 and No. 55 selections to the Houston Rockets in exchange for the No. 39 and No. 53 picks, along with a 2029 second-round selection originally belonging to the Sacramento Kings.The move further highlights the Knicks' strategy of accumulating future assets while maintaining financial flexibility. It also capped another busy chapter in a draft week that saw New York execute multiple trades and ultimately move away from holding a first-round selection despite entering the event with the No. 24 overall pick.The transaction represented the latest step in a draft strategy centered on maximizing value rather than targeting a specific prospect. By moving down eight spots from No. 31 to No. 39 and shifting from No. 55 to No. 53, New York secured an additional future second-round asset while retaining multiple selections on Day 2.The Knicks entered the draft as one of the league's most active teams. Their activity began with a trade involving the Los Angeles Lakers, where New York moved the No.24 pick for the No. 25 selection and cash considerations.That was followed by another deal with the Dallas Mavericks. After selecting Spanish guard Sergio De Larrea at No. 25, the Knicks immediately traded his rights to Dallas in exchange for the No. 30 pick and two future second-round selections. New York then flipped that pick to the Phoenix Suns, receiving the No. 47 selection, the draft rights to Melvin Ajinca, two additional second-round picks and cash considerations.According to SNY NBA Insider Ian Begley, the latest trade could carry financial significance beyond the draft board. Begley noted that "by trading out of first pick in second round of draft, the Knicks have positioned themselves to find players more likely to accept rookie minimum deal via second round pick exception."The flurry of transactions comes just weeks after New York captured the 2026 NBA championship and entered an offseason focused on balancing contention with long-term roster management.Begley previously reported that the Knicks' series of draft-day moves helped save approximately $3.4 million as the organization works to remain below the NBA's second apron for the 2026-27 season. That financial breathing room could prove valuable as the front office evaluates roster upgrades and contract decisions throughout the summer.Meanwhile, Houston wasted no time using the newly acquired No. 31 pick on Ohio State guard Bruce Thornton, the Buckeyes' all-time leading scorer. The selection gave the Rockets an immediate opportunity to add backcourt depth, while the Knicks continued stockpiling assets that could become useful in future trades or roster-building efforts.
Article Summary: The New York Knicks traded the No. 31 and No. 55 selections to the Houston Rockets. New York also received a 2029 second-round pick from the Sacramento Kings. The Knicks entered the draft as one of the league's most active teams.

Article Title: “The first one was f**king devastating”: Dwyane Wade’s wife Gabrielle Union breaks silence on the emotional toll of miscarriage

Dwyane Wade’s wife Gabrielle Union expresses the pain she felt after her first miscarriage



Dwyane Wade’s wife Gabrielle Union opens up about the anger she felt towards her husband as she experienced miscarriages

Which is why I think I couldn’t tell you exactly how many [miscarriages I had], because it’s just one long loss.”Gabrielle Union continued, “The first one is very vivid; everything else after that is just kind of like, numbing, just long, numbing, numb pain. Article Title: JPMorgan exec fired after viral Knicks parade video shows bizarre trash-can stunt
Article Text:
Angie Báez was reportedly fired by JPMorgan Chase after a viral Knicks parade video showed a woman dumping trash from a themed bin and taking it. (Images via Twitter and Getty)

Angie Báez’s viral Knicks parade video turned a celebration into a workplace problem

JPMorgan acted fast, but New York officials say no charges had been filed

Angie Báez left the New York Knicks’ championship parade with more than a memory. According to the New York Post, video showed a woman in Knicks gear dumping trash from a limited-edition blue-and-orange bin onto a Manhattan sidewalk, then walking away with the receptacle.The Post identified the woman as Báez, 40, an executive director at JPMorgan Chase. The bank fired her after the footage surfaced, according to the outlet. A JPMorgan Chase spokesperson told the Post, “This employee is no longer with the company.”The Knicks held their championship parade on June 18 after beating the San Antonio Spurs in five games to win their first NBA title in 53 years. The parade should have been the story. Instead, one trash can became the internet’s main character.Clips posted online showed Báez dressed in Knicks colors while emptying the themed bin onto the street. Additional footage showed her riding the subway with the bin, according to the Post.TMZ also reported that JPMorgan Chase fired Báez after reviewing the online footage. The Post first reported her firing.Báez had been promoted more than a year ago to executive director of community and industry engagement for card and connected commerce at JPMorgan Chase, according to her LinkedIn profile cited by the Post.The outlet also reported that she previously worked as executive director of diversity, equity, and inclusion at The Infatuation, a New York-based restaurant review site acquired by Chase. Earlier in her career, Báez held diversity and inclusion roles at Squarespace, Saks Fifth Avenue, Saks Off 5th, and Hudson’s Bay, according to online biographical information cited by the Post.Sources told the Post that Báez attended the parade in her personal capacity. She could not be reached by the outlet for comment Tuesday.The video created a simple problem for JPMorgan Chase. The conduct happened outside work, but the footage was public, easy to identify, and tied to a senior employee at one of the country’s biggest banks.The New York City Department of Sanitation did not soften its response. The agency told the Post, “Dumping trash onto the street and stealing public property for your own personal use are both illegal, antisocial behaviors, and not what New Yorkers do. On top of all that, doing both on camera is incredibly stupid.”The Post reported that under New York City law, theft of property valued under $1,000 is typically treated as petit larceny, a Class A misdemeanor. The littering could bring separate penalties.Still, the case had not moved into criminal charges as of the Post’s reporting. The NYPD said on June 20 that it had not received any complaints connected to the incident, and Báez had not been charged with a crime.That makes the ending unusually modern. No charge was needed for the video to matter. No courtroom was needed for the consequence.
Article Summary: Angie Báez, an executive director at JPMorgan Chase, was reportedly fired after a viral video surfaced. The video showed a woman in Knicks gear dumping trash from a limited-edition blue-and-orange bin onto a Manhattan sidewalk. The NYPD said on June 20 that it had not received any complaints connected to the incident.

Article Title: Not just Japan: Rui Hachimura wants Asia's basketball dream to grow bigger
Article Text:
Rui Hachimura (Photo by NBA Rising Stars Invitational)

TimesofIndia.com in SINGAPORE:

Thinking beyond Japan

NBA stars under one roof (Photo by NBA Rising Stars Invitational)

The challenge remains bigger than talent



Asia's best talents in action (Photo by NBA Rising Stars Invitational)

Building pathways

Lauren Jackson (Photo by NBA Rising Stars Invitational)

Mitch Richmond (Photo by NBA Rising Stars Invitational)

A tournament and a question

The lone Indian representatives (Photo by NBA Rising Stars Invitational)

By mid-morning, the courts inside the OCBC Arena had already settled into their rhythm.On one end, South Korea's Kyungbock High School looked every bit the early favourites, their players physically bigger and noticeably more imposing than most of their opponents. Singapore, with four teams spread across the boys' and girls' categories, found the going tougher against more established basketball programmes.Around the arena, coaches barked instructions, and teenagers from across Asia shared courts that, for the next week, would become the centre of the NBA 's ambitions in the region.The facilities are first class, the organisation unmistakably NBA. But what stood out most on the opening day of the Rising Stars Invitational wasn't necessarily the basketball itself.It was the diversity. Players from Japan, China, South Korea, India, the Philippines and Australia, each carrying different basketball traditions and ambitions, all arriving in Singapore with the same dream.For some, it is about winning. For others, simply being here is part of something bigger: Opportunity.For Rui Hachimura, perhaps more than anybody else, that word carries special meaning.The opportunity he wished he had.The Los Angeles Lakers forward is not just Japan's biggest basketball export. In 2019, Hachimura became the first Japanese player to be selected in the first round of the NBA Draft when the Washington Wizards picked him ninth overall.The 27-year-old, who won three consecutive national high school championships before moving to Gonzaga University, remains one of the few NBA players from Asia and has increasingly embraced the responsibility that comes with it."You know, I think when I was a kid, I always want to play overseas," Hachimura said on the sidelines of the event."I think this kind of opportunity is very important. I don't know how long this has existed, but when I was in high school, I don't think it existed. So that's why it makes sense."Looking around the arena and watching teenagers from across Asia compete under the NBA banner, Hachimura admitted he recognised a younger version of himself."Yeah, of course," he said when asked whether he saw himself in these players."When I was in high school, I got invited [to the] like [of] Jordan Brand Classic, NBA Academy and all that. But those are times that I actually go and learn myself. I can see my level and my goal.""So these kind of stuff is going to help them. Especially in Asia, there are a lot of kids, a lot of players that want to be in the NBA. So this is really good stuff."And perhaps that was the sentiment echoed most frequently throughout the day: Pathways.For years, Asian basketball has searched for figures capable of changing perceptions.China had Yao Ming, an eight-time NBA All-Star and the first overall pick in the 2002 Draft. Japan found its breakthrough star in Hachimura, who has since been joined by Yuta Watanabe and Yuki Kawamura in carrying the country's basketball aspirations.But for Hachimura himself, the conversation has moved beyond national boundaries."Especially for me, I think I have a responsibility. My country Japan, but also the whole Asia," he said."I think we have a lot of potential, but I think there are few opportunities. That's why I decided to come here and try to help the whole Asia continent to bring more players to the NBA."Later, when asked what success for Asian basketball would look like a decade from now, Hachimura again returned to the same theme."I don't want to talk about Japan," he said, adding."I think about whole Asia. That's my vision.""I want to inspire those kids and I want to have more kids from Asia be NBA players and do their thing. I'm thinking how I can help with that."For Hachimura, physical disadvantages are no longer excuses. "There is always this disadvantage for us," he said."But I think there are a lot of ways to prove that. You guys see Yuki Kawamura. He's literally this height and he competed every day, every game.""You just got to believe in yourself and find a way."More interestingly, Hachimura believes the challenge is not purely physical."It's the personality too," he said. "Basketball is an interesting sport. It's a team sport but a lot of times it's individual too. Showing what you can do is really important.""That's the things that I had to learn when I got to Gonzaga [University]. They always told me I play like I'm shy or something.""But you can't be like that. You always got to be aggressive. You got to be the guy trying to change the whole game. I think the Asians don't have that, so I think I have to teach that."Three-time WNBA MVP Lauren Jackson, widely regarded as one of the greatest players in the history of women's basketball and a central figure in Australia's rise as a global force, believes opportunity is becoming the defining theme of this generation.A Basketball Hall of Famer and one of the faces of the NBA's Her Time To Play initiative, Jackson has seen the women's game evolve over more than two decades."I think women's basketball is driving it globally," Jackson said. "I think there's no doubt that the WNBA has definitely lifted the stakes.""We're seeing the recognition because of women's basketball and it's such a great time for women's sport."For Jackson, tournaments like the Rising Stars Invitational are important because they create visibility."This tournament creates an opportunity for all the other countries to really have a pathway to the elite," she said."They can see their way to the NBA and that's something that's never been done before, particularly in the Asia-Pacific."Mitch Richmond, a six-time NBA All-Star, 2002 NBA champion with the Los Angeles Lakers and a member of the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame, has witnessed the league's international expansion from close quarters.Part of the famed trio at Golden State Warriors alongside Tim Hardaway and Chris Mullin, Richmond believes the NBA's efforts to globalise the game have been decades in the making."I saw it coming," Richmond said of basketball's global growth."When I was playing, the NBA always had me go overseas and they were really trying to expand.""To see it all come to fruition right now, where the NBA is growing in so many places and continues to grow globally, it's really a sight to see."And when asked about countries like India, where cricket remains dominant, Richmond preferred patience over urgency."You have to start somewhere. You have to take those baby steps. It's about teaching and learning. You have to get the youth excited."The lone Indian representatives, Velammal International School, will begin their campaign on Tuesday.Their task is difficult. The gap in infrastructure and basketball culture between India and some of Asia's established powers remains evident.Yet, that is perhaps the larger significance of this week. Because for Hachimura, success is no longer measured by what Japan achieves."I have a bigger goal now," he said. "Not only Japan but whole Asia."Inside the OCBC Arena, surrounded by teenagers from across the region, that vision no longer feels entirely distant.And for countries still waiting for their breakthrough moment, that might be reason enough to keep believing.
Article Summary: Rui Hachimura is the first Japanese player to be selected in the first round of the NBA Draft. The 27-year-old is one of the few NBA players from Asia. He has increasingly embraced the responsibility that comes with it. He wants to inspire more kids from Asia to be NBA players.
